task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the InVesalius 3 application window and interface in the screenshots.

task_completion.description: Verify the CT Cranium DICOM series is loaded
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for medical imaging (CT scans of a skull/cranium) displayed in the main viewing panels of InVesalius.
Locate the specific panel displaying the Axial plane (typically top-left, showing a top-down cross-section of the skull, often labeled 'Axial').

task_completion.description: Verify navigation to a different axial slice
task_completion.visual_evidence: Compare the initial and final screenshots. Look for a clear change in the anatomical cross-section shown in the Axial view, a change in the slice number/index displayed in the Axial panel's overlay, or a movement in the scrollbar position for that panel.

task_completion.description: Ensure the axial view remains visible at the end of the task
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the final screenshot, verify that the Axial view panel is still open, unobstructed, and actively displaying a slice.

integrity.description: Agent used the GUI to navigate slices
integrity.visual_evidence: Verify that the slice change was achieved through GUI interactions (e.g., mouse cursor over the scrollbar, slice number input box, or view panel) rather than terminal scripts.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual InVesalius 3 application
integrity.visual_evidence: Ensure the interface is genuinely InVesalius 3 and not a generic image viewer or web browser displaying static images.

integrity.description: Agent did not close the axial view during the process
integrity.visual_evidence: Verify that the axial view panel was not closed, minimized, or completely replaced by other views (like a maximized 3D view) during the trajectory.
